hawkish_phrases: deflation risks identified earlier in the year...had largely disappeared
the recovery was firming and becoming more broad-based
recalibration reflected the state contingency of the APP
a few members could not support either of the two options...in view of their well-known general scepticism regarding the APP
possible adverse side effects from further sovereign asset purchases
dovish_phrases: extension of the asset purchase programme beyond March 2017 seemed warranted
there was as yet insufficient progress towards a sustained adjustment in the path of inflation
underlying inflation...continued to lack a convincing upward trend
preserving a very substantial degree of monetary accommodation necessary
if the outlook became less favourable...the Governing Council intended to increase the programme in terms of size and/or duration
summary: The Governing Council decided to extend the APP beyond March 2017 at a reduced monthly pace of €60 billion (down from €80 billion) until December 2017, with broad (not unanimous) support, reflecting improved growth and reduced deflation risks but insufficient progress toward sustained inflation adjustment.
